design: Total RNA is isolated from the bee in the difference phase. Depending on the research focus, specific RNA types may be enriched or depleted. RNA is often fragmented to a suitable size range. It is then reverse-transcribed into complementary DNA (cDNA). During library construction, adapters are added to the cDNA fragments to facilitate sequencing on NGS platforms. Maintaining strand-specific information during this step is crucial for accurately identifying overlapping genes and novel transcripts.
